brand.name: My Career Journey
brand.tagline: Plan your path after school, one step at a time!

common.comingSoonNote: This page is a placeholder for now — real content will be added here.
common.machineTranslatedNote: This page was machine-translated and may contain errors. For anything important — exam dates, fees, safety advice — check the official English source too.

copyPrompt.hint: Tip: copy the prompt and paste it into a free AI chatbot (Gemini, ChatGPT, Copilot) for the best answer — a long prompt can get cut short if you only open the Google link. Always double-check exam dates and fees on the official websites.

home.heroTitle: What do you want to do after school? 🤔
home.heroSubtitle: Get a step-by-step plan for the career you want — colleges, exams, costs and dates, all in one place!
home.feature1Body: Every step in order — which class, which exam, which year.
home.feature2Body: Compare your options and filter them by cost: free, low, mid or high.
home.feature3Body: We link to the official site so you can confirm everything yourself.
home.exploreBody: Browse our free guides, or make a DIY plan!
home.disclaimer: Dates, fees and rules change every year. Check the official site before you act!

questions.goal: What do you want to become?

safety.blocked: Please enter a career or job you're interested in.

safety.distressTitle: You don't have to face this alone
safety.distressBody: It sounds like you might be going through a really hard time. If you're feeling distressed or thinking about harming yourself, please reach out — talking to someone helps. In India you can call these free, confidential helplines any time:

done.title: Your journey is ready
done.lead: Here's how we'll shape your plan:
done.sampleNote: We'll generate a grounded plan for you and check official links before showing specifics. Specifics stay marked "verify" until a human reviews them.
done.generating: Generating your plan — this can take a moment while we check official sources…
done.generateError: Couldn't generate your plan. Please try again.
done.exploreNote: Exploration mode ("help me explore") isn't available here yet — go back and pick a specific career to get a full plan, or plan it yourself.

generatingChat.footnote: This usually takes a minute or two while we check official sources. You can keep this open.
steps: Got it — let me put your plan together. 🧭
Checking the latest exam dates and deadlines…
Almost ready — adding the finishing touches…
generatingChat.promptTitle: Get your plan from a free AI tool instead
generatingChat.promptLead: Copy this ready-made prompt and paste it into any free AI chatbot — or search it on Google. Your career and class are already filled in.
generatingChat.fallbackLead: No need to wait — you can still plan this yourself. Our step-by-step guide shows how to research any career and check the dates, fees, and eligibility on the official websites.

journey.candidateBannerTitle: AI-generated — This page contains AI-generated information.
journey.candidateBanner: A computer wrote this plan, so some details may be wrong. Always check dates, fees, and exam rules on the official websites before you depend on them!

actions.copyManual: Copy this link to bookmark or share your plan:
actions.copyPromptLead: Paste this into any free AI chatbot, or search it on Google, to regenerate this plan or dig deeper — your career and class are already filled in.

notFound.title: This plan is being refreshed
notFound.body: We couldn't find a saved copy of this plan just now. You can generate a fresh one — it keeps the same shareable link.
notFound.badLink: This plan link looks incomplete. Try starting a new plan.
notFound.error: Couldn't generate a plan right now. Please try again.

journey.fallbackTitle: Missed a deadline? You still have options

filters.noneMatch: Nothing matches this cost filter. Try a different bucket.

download.heading: Download timeline
download.approxNote: Approximate timing — a planning horizon, not a fixed date. Verify exact dates on official sites before acting.
download.calendarCappedNote: Calendar shows about the next 2 years; later steps are in the printable plan.

cvTemplates.title: CV / Resume Templates
cvTemplates.intro: Free, simple CV tools for students. Fill in a short form to make your CV, or download a blank one to fill in yourself. No work experience needed!
cvTemplates.quickIntro: Fill in the boxes and download your finished CV. Pick Word to keep editing it, or PDF to send as-is. Empty boxes are left out.
cvTemplates.privacyNote: Everything you type stays on your device — nothing is uploaded or saved.
cvTemplates.blankIntro: Download a clean, one-page template and fill it in your own way. Get a Word file you can edit, or a text file that opens on any phone.

student-no-experience.name: Student CV — no work experience
student-no-experience.guide: Put your name and contact details at the top. You don't need job experience — school, projects and activities all count. List your latest school or college first. Under Projects & activities, write things you've really done — a class project, a poster, helping at a family shop, a sports team — one short line each. Under Skills, add the languages you speak and any computer or practical skills. Keep it to one page. Use simple words.

first-internship.name: First internship CV
first-internship.guide: This one is for your first internship or part-time job. Put your contact details at the top. Add a two-line About me: what you're studying and the internship you want. Change these two lines for each company. List your education, latest first. Under Experience, add any work, volunteering or freelance task — even unpaid — with one line on what you did. Use Projects for course or personal work, and Skills for tools and languages. Keep it to one page and save it as a PDF before applying.

cvTemplates.buildersIntro: Prefer to build it on screen? These tools are free to use:

builders.googleDocs: Free with any Google account — open Google Docs, choose Template gallery, then a Resume template. Download as PDF.
builders.canva: Free templates you fill in by clicking. A free account lets you download your CV as a PDF.
builders.flowcv: Clean templates you fill in online. Free PDF download — no payment to export.
builders.indeed: Build a résumé on Indeed and apply to jobs with it. Free to create and download.

cvTemplates.buildersAvoid: Some sites let you build for free, then charge you to download the PDF. Check that the download is free before you start.

freeResources.title: Free Learning Resources
freeResources.intro: All free! Tap a filter to find what you need.
freeResources.tip: Most of these work on a basic phone. Start with one, not ten.

interviewPrep.title: Interview Prep
interviewPrep.intro: Your first interview feels scary — that's normal! Nobody expects a student to be perfect. They just want to see that you're honest, keen to learn, and a little prepared. You can get ready for almost everything below.

method.heading: How to answer most questions
method.intro: When asked “tell me about a time you…”, use 3 steps:
method.exampleQ: “Tell me about a time you worked in a team.”
method.example: Our class had to organise a fundraiser (situation). I was put in charge of collecting and tracking the money (action). We raised ₹8,000 and I made sure every rupee was accounted for (result).

remember.heading: Remember:
remember.body: being honest and keen to learn is more important than being “perfect”. Just relax and give it your best!

findJobs.title: Find Jobs & Internships
findJobs.intro: Don't know where to start? Here's where to look, how to apply, and how to stay safe.

audience.heading: Which one is you right now?

scams.heading: Stay safe from fake jobs
scams.intro: Some fake “jobs” go after students looking for their first job. A few quick checks keep you safe:

quickStart.label: Quick start
quickStart.p3: account, and a simple CV. That's enough to start applying today!

planItYourself.title: Plan It Yourself
planItYourself.intro: No ready-made plan for you yet? Use this blank plan and the short guide below to research any career yourself!
planItYourself.templateIntro: Fill in each step for the career you have in mind.
planItYourself.downloadNote: Print or Save as PDF — your plan includes everything you type below.
planItYourself.monthlyIntro: Pick one or two things to do each month — an exam to sign up for, a skill to build, someone to talk to.
planItYourself.monthlyPlaceholder: What will you do this month?
templateSteps: The qualification or degree it needs:
The exam(s) involved and roughly when they happen:
Two colleges or institutes near me:
Approximate cost, and where I confirmed it:
planItYourself.guideTitle: How to research any career on your own
guideSteps: Search the career plus "official" to find the governing body or institute.
Find the real website — look for .gov.in, .org, or the institute's own site.
Look for the "admissions" or "apply" page; that's where real dates and fees live.
Check the fees against a second source. If a number looks too low or too high, dig deeper.
Don't pay an "agent" for something you can do yourself for free.

footer.rights: A free career guide for Indian students. Check official sites for exam dates and fees.

about.title: The story behind this website!
about.p1: Once upon a time, at a career mentorship session held by my organization for 12th grade students, a girl told me she wanted guidance on becoming a Cabin Crew member at IndiGo, another asked me how she can become a Fashion Designer, while another wanted to become a Chartered Accountant.
about.p2: The only path I was well versed with was the one I took to become an engineer; I didn't have the answers to their questions, so I used search engines and AI tools to get more information about other career options, answer their questions and create planned journeys for them.
about.p3: I spent some time thinking about this and built a free website to help high-school-students plan their career journeys. Besides some pre-configured, common career options, you can create a plan for yourself using an LLM-powered chat. I've also linked some free resources which can help people at different stages of their career journeys, even after they have graduated from school or college. You can switch between English, Hindi, and a couple of regional languages.
about.p4: Feel free to share this with younger children and high school students, and would love to get your feedback if you try it out!
